description:
## Parent Task Context (Already Completed)

> **Note:** The following parent task has already been completed. This context is provided to help you understand the background and requirements for this sub-task. Do not re-implement the parent task - focus only on the new sub-task described below.

### Action Item Assignment & Tracking

**Description:** Automatically extract action items from meeting transcripts and assign them to participants. Track completion status with due dates and send reminders via email.

**Rationale:** Action items die in meeting notes. Surfacing them as trackable tasks with ownership closes the loop between discussion and execution.

**Summary:** ## Summary: Automatically extract action items from meeting transcripts

### Changes Implemented
- **Database schema**: Added `action_items` table with `action_item_status` enum to Drizzle schema, supporting fields for title, description, assignee, status (open/in_progress/completed/cancelled), priority, due date, completion tracking, and reminder tracking. Linked to both `users` and `webhook_key_points` tables.
- **Action Items CRUD API** (`/api/action-items`): Full REST API for listing (with status/meeting filters), creating, updating, and deleting action items. Auto-sets `completedAt` timestamp when status changes to completed.
- **AI Extraction API** (`/api/action-items/extract`): Uses Claude (claude-sonnet-4) to analyze meeting transcripts and key points, extracting structured action items with title, description, assignee (from participants), priority, and suggested due dates.
- **Email Reminders API** (`/api/action-items/remind`): Sends reminder emails via Resend for overdue and due-today action items, with HTML-formatted email listing all items needing attention. Marks items as reminded to avoid duplicate notifications.
- **Action Items Page** (`/action-items`): Full management UI with status filter tabs (All/Open/In Progress/Completed/Cancelled), inline editing of status/priority/assignee/due date, completion toggle checkboxes, delete functionality, overdue highlighting, and a "Send Reminders" button.
- **Meeting Detail Drawer**: Added "Extract Action Items" button and a third "Action Items" tab showing extracted items for each meeting with status, priority, assignee, and due date info.
- **Navigation**: Added "Action Items" link with clipboard-check icon to the sidebar navigation.
- **Validation schemas**: Added Zod schemas for `createActionItem` and `updateActionItem` with proper field validation.
- **TypeScript types**: Added `ActionItem` and `ActionItemStatus` types to the shared types file.

### Files Modified
- `lib/db/schema.ts` - Added `actionItemStatusEnum` and `actionItems` table
- `types/index.ts` - Added `ActionItem` and `ActionItemStatus` types
- `lib/validators/schemas.ts` - Added action item validation schemas
- `app/api/action-items/route.ts` - **NEW** - List/create action items
- `app/api/action-items/[id]/route.ts` - **NEW** - Get/update/delete single action item
- `app/api/action-items/extract/route.ts` - **NEW** - AI extraction from meeting transcripts
- `app/api/action-items/remind/route.ts` - **NEW** - Email reminders for due/overdue items
- `app/(app)/action-items/page.tsx` - **NEW** - Action items management page
- `components/meeting/MeetingDetailDrawer.tsx` - Added extract button and action items tab
- `components/ui/SideNav.tsx` - Added Action Items nav link with icon

### Notes for Developer
- The `action_items` table has already been pushed to the Neon database via `drizzle-kit push`
- AI extraction uses `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` (same model as meeting search) and requires the `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var
- Email reminders require `RESEND_API_KEY` and `RESEND_EMAIL` env vars (same as password reset)
- The remind endpoint can be called manually via the UI button, or wired to a cron job for automated daily reminders
- Action items are scoped per-user via the auth session
- The build compiles cleanly with zero TypeScript errors

### Verification Status
- Verified with Playwright: 6 tests passing
  - All 4 API endpoints correctly return 401 for unauthenticated requests
  - Action items page renders and redirects to login when not authenticated
  - Login page loads with expected form elements
- Full Next.js production build completes successfully with all routes registered
- Test files cleaned up after verification

---

## Task Description

When action items are extracted from a transcript, they should be saved. Right now, when I click on "Extract action items", the action items are generated, but then if I go to dismiss the dialog and then come back to the dialog, the action items disappear. On the action item tab, we can instead implement this as one of the Kanban boards, so we need a way to specify which board to add action items to. To facilitate this, we should have a default Kanban board.  I think we can get rid of the action-items page, and then I want, in an upcoming feature, to automatically extract the action items and assign them to the Kanban board versus having to click an extract button.
title: Create default Kanban board for action items

## Task Description

When action items are extracted from a transcript, they should be saved. Right now, when I click on "Extract action items", the action items are generated, but then if I go to dismiss the dialog and then come back to the dialog, the action items disappear. On the action item tab, we can instead implement this as one of the Kanban boards, so we need a way to specify which board to add action items to. To facilitate this, we should have a default Kanban board.  I think we can get rid of the action-items page, and then I want, in an upcoming feature, to automatically extract the action items and assign them to the Kanban board versus having to click an extract button.

summary:
## Summary: Action Item Board Integration & Persistence

### Changes Implemented
- **Database schema**: Added `defaultBoardId` (nullable UUID) column to the `users` table in `lib/db/schema.ts`, allowing users to set a default Kanban board for action item extraction. Schema pushed to Neon database via `drizzle-kit push`.
- **Default board settings API** (`/api/settings/default-board`): New REST endpoint with GET (fetch user's default board) and PUT (set default board) handlers, following existing auth patterns.
- **Extract API enhancement** (`/api/action-items/extract`): Now accepts an optional `boardId` parameter. When provided, extracted action items are also created as Kanban cards on the board's first column (by position), with proper board ownership verification and position calculation (multiples of 1024).
- **MeetingDetailDrawer update**: Added board selector dropdown next to the "Extract Action Items" button. Users can select which board to send extracted items to, with a "Set default" link to persist their choice. The selected board defaults to the user's saved default board. Action items are persisted in the database and loaded from the API when re-opening the drawer, fixing the issue where items disappeared.
- **Navigation cleanup**: Removed "Action Items" nav link from sidebar since the standalone page is no longer needed — action items are now managed through meetings (extraction) and Kanban boards (tracking).
- **Action Items page removal**: Deleted `app/(app)/action-items/page.tsx` and its directory. The action items API routes (`/api/action-items/*`) are preserved as they're still used by the drawer and the remind endpoint.

### Files Modified
- `lib/db/schema.ts` - Added `defaultBoardId` field to users table
- `app/api/settings/default-board/route.ts` - **NEW** - Default board settings API
- `app/api/action-items/extract/route.ts` - Added `boardId` param and Kanban card creation
- `components/meeting/MeetingDetailDrawer.tsx` - Added board selector, default board management
- `components/ui/SideNav.tsx` - Removed Action Items nav link and unused ActionItemsIcon
- `app/(app)/action-items/page.tsx` - **DELETED** - Standalone action items page removed

### Notes for Developer
- The `default_board_id` column has been pushed to the Neon database via `drizzle-kit push`
- When extracting action items with a board selected, cards are created in the board's first column (sorted by position). If the board has no columns, cards won't be created (no error thrown).
- The action items API routes are preserved for: fetching meeting-specific items in the drawer, the remind endpoint for email reminders, and CRUD operations
- Build compiles cleanly with zero TypeScript errors
- The upcoming automatic extraction feature can use the `defaultBoardId` from the users table to determine where to place cards without user interaction
